title: Use semi-colon as the field separator for internal volumes-from inspect annotation
body:
The current field separator comma of the inspect annotation conflicts with the mount options of --volumes-from as the mount options itself can be comma separated.

When a volumes-from value is ctr1:z,ro, the container inspect shows the following:
```
               "VolumesFrom": [
                    "f91dc2fa04d6cd3997c7b3f5c300ceca39bc37e5c5dbb847e89d342d8141c663:z",
                    "ro"
               ],
```
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
tests are failing to build.
/approve
This is a breaking change, but I don't think it will affect many (any) users.

@vikas-goel: Cannot trigger testing until a trusted user reviews the PR and leaves an `/ok-to-test` message.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/21497#issuecomment-1925440887):

>/retest


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Why should the inspect field include the volume options in the first place? Wouldn't it be better to just display the ids and ignore ignore the options? Otherwise users of such output would be forced to always parse the options in the string to get the actual id which I doubt many would know about.

> Why should the inspect field include the volume options in the first place? Wouldn't it be better to just display the ids and ignore ignore the options? Otherwise users of such output would be forced to always parse the options in the string to get the actual id which I doubt many would know about.

I believe this question is not intended for me as I am just fixing the existing broken functionality.

@vikas-goel Well the question is do you care about the options included?
I would rather just not include the options at all but maybe other maintainers see that differently.

> @vikas-goel Well the question is do you care about the options included?
> I would rather just not include the options at all but maybe other maintainers see that differently.

I don’t care about the ‘VolumesFrom’ field.
Let's just split the output with ":" and just show the ID.

> Let's just split the output with ":" and just show the ID.

That may result in a new issue. The kube generate will produce incorrect annotation in the yaml.
Ok, If this is set in kube generate then it needs the options obviously to make sure it works the same after play.
